Headline Ideas

* 15 Real World Ahrefs MCP Use Cases for SEOs & Digital Marketers  
* The Ahrefs MCP Server Won Me Over: Here’s 15 Ways It Can Do the Same for You  
* 15 Reasons the Ahrefs MCP Server Became Part of My Marketing Toolkit  
* Here’s 15 (+1) Practical Use Cases for the New Ahrefs MCP Server  
* New Tool: The Ahrefs MCP Server Is Ready for Your Marketing Prompts

With the recent launch of our remote MCP server, anyone can now chat directly with Ahrefs data to get actionable SEO insights.

MCP, which stands for Model Context Protocol, is an open standard which companies can use to integrate their external tools and data with compatible AI assistants. 

Imagine you’ve recently launched a new clothing brand and want to find eCommerce stores ranking well for the types of products you’ll sell— a common goal before reverse-engineering what makes them successful. 

The standard Ahrefs interface enables you to look up any site to uncover things like the keywords they’re ranking for, their most linked-to content, and how overall search traffic is trending. 

Our new MCP server, which integrates with popular AI assistants like ChatGPT and Claude, offers the ability to automate that and countless other processes, to get insights at scale.

Ask for advice as if you’re talking with a friend, and in this case, we now have a list of potential competitors to start investigating further. 

![][image1]

Available on Ahrefs Lite, Standard, Advanced and Enterprise plans, you’ll now be able to tap directly into our API without needing to know how to code.

The only problem you might face is, “What should I ask next?”.

This guide aims to answer that very question.

## \<H2\>My best advice for using the Ahrefs MCP server to improve your daily marketing workflow\</h2\>

Whenever I’m working on SEO projects for clients, I typically use multiple tools to perform similar tasks.

For example, I crawl websites using the Ahrefs Site Audit tool, Screaming Frog, *and* custom software. 

My process for keyword research includes Ahrefs, Google Trends, Google Autosuggest, and manual checks of search results. 

I learned early on that some of my best insights come from having multiple perspectives on related data. When you’re working on sites with tens of thousands or even millions of pages, it’s possible to miss finer details in one tool that stand out in another. 

I view using the Ahrefs MCP server in a similar way: **I gain new insights thanks to a different perspective that AI provides, and as a bonus, it enables me to scale operations that I already know how to perform.**

It won't (and shouldn’t) fully replace all manual checks and human intelligence.

At times, you’ll receive recommendations you’re already aware of, and like many AI assistants, it might make things sound simpler than they are in reality.

But it can also tell you about a cluster of uncommon keywords a site is ranking for that gives you an idea of how you can compete with your own take.

It will remind you that specific rankings a competitor has picked up are seasonal, and that it might be good to prepare content for the “peak” coming in a few months.

It will inform you about the small niche site you initially overlooked that has ramped up content production in recent months which, from an SEO perspective, is getting results.

And if you’re still not convinced, chatting directly with Ahrefs data is just plain fun. 

### \<h3\>A Brief Note on the Set Up Process & Prompting\</h3\>

Our [MCP help docs](https://docs.ahrefs.com/docs/mcp/reference/introduction) provide the full details on how to link your Ahrefs account to your preferred AI assistant.

There are guides to five popular AI tools including Claude, ChatGPT and Microsoft Copilot Studio. Alternatively, you can use our remote connection URL directly: https://api.ahrefs.com/mcp/mcp.

While you can get the same great functionality from the ChatGPT integration as Claude, MCP connectors are in beta in ChatGPT, and that’s noticeable at times. 

I say this as a paying ChatGPT customer for years and a Claude one for just a few weeks, I much prefer the experience in Claude. 

Tools don’t always know to use the Ahrefs MCP server versus searching the web, so it’s highly recommended that you’re specific about that, starting requests with “Use the Ahrefs MCP server” or “Using the Ahrefs MCP server,...”.

Assume that I may have had to add that to the following prompts if they didn’t route through the Ahrefs MCP connection automatically. 

## \<h2\>15 prompt ideas across three levels of insights\</h2\>

Simple prompts can provide actionable responses in seconds, while others that require more data (such as analyzing the backlink profile of multiple sites) can take a few minutes to process.

Generally, the more in-depth your request, the more units you’ll consume and the longer it will take to finish your response.

We’ll get into some tips and tricks after you’ve seen what’s possible, but I should advise upfront that at any time you can go to Account \> Limits & Usage to see how many units you’ve currently consumed. 

### \<h3\>Level one: simple prompts to get started\</h3\>

Response time: Typically under two minutes. 

\<h3\>1. Identify sites growing organic search traffic in 2025\</h3\>

Prompt: Using this list of 10 sites in my niche, tell me which have grown organic search traffic from January 2025 to September 2025: \[Domains 1-10 e.g. [Clickup.com](http://Clickup.com)\].

![][image2]  
Note: These graphics only include a single part of a more detailed response. 

Description: Your AI assistant will quickly look up estimated organic search traffic to each specific site for the timeframe suggested. It will likely add commentary on overall growth percentages and any interesting stand-out months for a particular company.

\<h3\>2. Discover terms competitors rank on the first page of Google for\</h3\>

Prompt: Tell me about the rankings \[Competitor\] has on the first page of Google that \[My Site\] doesn't have first-page rankings for.

Description: Here the MCP will query the site-explorer-organic-competitors endpoint for both sites. You can also use Ahrefs' \<a href=”https://app.ahrefs.com/competitive-analysis”\>Competitive Analysis tool\</a\> to manually research opportunities.

\<h3\>3. Find the most linked-to content on any site or subfolder\</h3\>

Prompt: What are the top 10 pages on \[industry blog\] ranked by how many links each has. Also include estimated organic search traffic for each. 

Description: I like to look up how specific directories are performing — not just domains as a whole. For instance, finding the most linked to Ahrefs.com/blog/ articles is very different to the Ahrefs domain as a whole.

\<h3\>4. Find organic search competitors for any site\</h3\>

Prompt: Give me a list of the closest organic search competitors for \[Competitor site e.g. Docusign.com\]. 

Description: The Ahrefs API has a specific endpoint to show organic search competitors ranked by how many common keywords each site shares. In the example above we'll get back insights on sites like PandaDoc, Dropbox and SignWell.

You can perform this action manually in the Ahrefs Dashboard by going to Site Explorer \> Organic Competitors.

\<h3\>5. Combine keyword research with blog post headline ideas\</h3\>

Prompt: My business sells \[product type e.g. coffee tables\], and I’m looking to research more blog post ideas. Help me find keywords that people use for research before making a purchase.

Description: This prompt will pull data directly from Ahrefs' keyword database, looking up informational, comparison, question-based and similar query types relevant to your described scenario.

### \<h2\>Level Two: Intermediate, More In-Depth Prompts\</h2\>

Response Time: Typically 2-10 minutes.

\<h3\>6. Uncover trending keywords (and why they're growing in popularity)\</h3\>

Prompt: I’ve covered \[my niche e.g. gardening\] with a lot of evergreen content angles. Are there any trending terms closely related to my industry that you could recommend considering? Preferably up to 20 terms with the potential to grow in volume in 2026\. 

![][image3]

Description: Ahrefs Keyword Explorer has valuable insights for each keyword like search volume over time, traffic potential and keyword difficulty. The MCP can utilize all of these via the API to offer suggestions.

\<h3\>7. Batch analyzse specific domain insights (like Organic Traffic and their Domain Rating)\</h3\>

Prompt: Here’s a list of 20 sites in my niche \[attach a file or enter the URLs\]. Please create a table that includes their Ahrefs DR, Organic Traffic, and the number of keywords they’re ranking in the top 3 positions for in search results. 

Description: It's often the case I'll have a list of sites to dive into that I've discovered through resaerch outside of Ahrefs, that I now want additional SEO-focused insights on. The MCP is one great way to get these details at scale.

\<h3\>8. Structure content outlines using insights from keyword research\</h3\>

Prompt: I’m writing a guide on \[topic e.g. the best backpacks for traveling\]. Conduct keyword research to help identify key topics I should cover, then structure an example article outline using your findings. Keep it natural, rather than overly keyword-focused.

Description: Combine keyword research with an AI assistant's understanding of how articles are typically structured. If you like, it can also highlight some of the top ranking pages for further research.

\<h3\>9. Discover the top-ranking sites across any group of keywords\</h3\>

Prompt: Across this list of 10 different keywords relevant to my niche, tell me which sites rank for the most terms and in the best positions. 

\[Include your keywords, such as:

\- Best times to post on TikTok  
\- Carousel post dimensions for LinkedIn\]

Description: This prompt is great for quickly discovering the top sites in niches you aren't already familiar with.

\<h3\>10. Find broken backlinks to any domain or URL path\</h3\>

Prompt: Find broken backlinks pointing to any URLs under the following subdirectory \[e.g.nytimes.com/wirecutter/\]. I'm specifically looking for individual URLs that receive the most links from other sites but don’t exist. Focus on high DR links, and just one per domain.

Description: Get insights from the 'Broken Backlinks' report we offer in Site Explorer. If you're familiar with the filters we have in place (like only returning \<em\>One link per domain\</em\>) then you can easily enhance your prompts.

### 

### \<h2\>Level Three: Advanced, Resource-Intensive Prompts\</h2\>

Response Time: Two to 10+ minutes. The bigger the request (i.e., the more keywords, search results, or sites to analyze), the longer it will take. 

\<h3\>11. Research international SEO expansion opportunities\</h3\>

Prompt: I run a \[business type, e.g. online fashion marketplace\] primarily for the US market. We're looking to expand internationally. Please find up to 10 examples of similar businesses that expanded into other countries. Outline what they were, and how their organic traffic is trending in those countries. 

![][image4]

Description: Certain industries like fashion will have competitors which have expanded into dozens of other countries. Here the goal is to see where they're performing well (and which countries they didn't target) to start the research process for future business decisions.

In this example, the MCP quickly checked international search traffic for brands such as UNIQLO, Shein, H\&M, ASOS, Nike and Mango.

\<h3\>12. Insights into competitor content strategies\</h3\>

Prompt: Analyze the top ten organic search competitors to \[Site e.g. AllRecipes.com\]. I want to get an idea of any unique, non-standard approaches they're taking to content creation and the terms they're targeting, outside of \[common topic, e.g. recipes\].

Description: The aim here is to first discover the content strategies across multiple sites to then be able to understand the commonalities and any interesting outliers. 

\<h3\>13. Overall site recommendations to increase organic search traffic\</h3\>

Prompt: You’re an SEO expert. Your job is to increase organic traffic to \[Site e.g. Chewy.com\] in a legitimate, whitehat way. You have access to the best SEO data on the planet via the Ahrefs MCP. Research the most relevant information you can about my site and the industry in which it operates. Offer any advice you feel is appropriate. 

Description: This example was inspired by \<a href="https://x.com/Marie\_Haynes"\>Marie Haynes\</a\>, who kindly messaged me (with permission to share) to say she was gaining some great insights from prompts along this line.

I think it perfectly fits into the ‘fun’ category, where it’s not super obvious what kind of response you’ll get, but potentially provides a new perspective on something you might have missed.

\<h3\>14. In-depth industry research based on specific rankings\</h3\>

Prompt: Provide a list of the top 20 keyphrases that \[Site e.g.GarageGymReviews.com\] is ranking first-page for, where the ‘People Also Ask’ SERP feature is present. Rank them by estimated search volume. Only include terms at least four words in length, and exclude any that are branded (i.e., mention \[phrase to ignore e.g. ‘Garage Gym Reviews’\]). Then tell me the top ten sites ranking overall for those terms combined.

Description: This prompt starts by identifying very specific rankings just one site has, then uses those terms to get a different perspective on organic competitors.

\<h3\>15. Analyze multiple backlink profiles at once\</h3\>

Prompt: Provide me a comprehensive overview of the backlink profile of these five websites I’m researching: \[Domain 1,2,3,4,5\]. Be sure to include the rate at which they’re picking up links from new domains. 

Description: I specifically like researching the rate at which competitors are picking up new backlinks compared. Analzying multiple sites at once helps you form a good overview as to how your brand stacks up.

### \<h3\>A Bonus Prompt Opportunity Only Available As Of This Week\</h3\>

The Ahrefs development team constantly adds new features to our API, and when they do, it means you can also gain additional insights via our MCP server.

We’ve just released the Page Explorer endpoint in APIv3 (technical details [here](https://docs.ahrefs.com/docs/api/site-audit/operations/get-a-page-explorer)), which provides page-level details from any websites you’ve used Ahrefs to crawl.

You can first use the Ahrefs MCP server to get a list of issues that apply to your most recent Site Audit crawl:

![][image5]

From there, you can use the new endpoint to explore specific issues, such as which URLs redirect, or all pages that return a 404 status code.

## Tips & Tricks for Getting the Most Out of the MCP as your Day-to-Day Marketing Assistant

**Especially for ChatGPT, make sure you mention the Ahrefs MCP server in your prompts**. 

As an example, a request for something around the top blogs in a certain niche can quickly trigger your AI assistant of choice to start searching the web.

Including  “Using the Ahrefs MCP server, please list the top blogs ranking for…” in your prompt will ensure it collects data directly from Ahrefs instead. 

**Be specific about the number of sites you’re looking to analyse, keyword suggestions you would like, and the date range you want insights from.** This way you can avoid getting too few insights, or too many that you consume unnecessary units. 

**Once connected, try asking your AI assistant of choice for inspiration on what to prompt.** This sounds meta, but it works: Explain the current situation of the projects you’re working on from a marketing perspective and what you’re struggling with. You can then see some example prompts which might help with what to ask. 

**While ChatGPT connectors are in beta, I’ve personally found Claude to be a more enjoyable experience.** I prefer ChatGPT’s design, but not when it comes to calling tools (like the Ahrefs MCP server).

**If you’re already familiar with a process to follow, be clear about the order of the task you want your AI assistant to follow.** If you typically look up the keywords a site is ranking for before you analyze its backlinks, prompt your assistant to do the same. 

**If you encounter any oddities (they’re rare), you can inspect the response to gain an idea of what might have gone wrong**. ChatGPT’s own documentation [recommends this step](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/developer-mode) to help debug any issues. It may be the case that your prompt could be improved, or you’ve asked for something we don’t have data on (like where a site ranks in DuckDuckGo). 

**You can easily check your current usage and limits via the Ahrefs dashboard**. Simply navigate to Accounts \> [Limits & Usage](https://app.ahrefs.com/account/limits-and-usage/web%20) and scroll down to the API units / mo section.  

**Don’t expect the MCP to replace all human intelligence (or our typical interface), but see it as an addition to those.** Some tasks are always going to be more enjoyable in a purpose-built UI than in a chat-like interaction.

But when scale and a new perspective are what you’re looking for, you might just wonder how you worked without the MCP before now. 

## Wrapping up

I didn’t initially expect to enjoy using the MCP server as much as I have. Now that it’s here, I keep finding new use cases to gain insights and increase productivity.

On a personal note, I’m honored to be writing my first-ever blog post here after recently joining the company.

I’ll be working with the Ahrefs marketing and product teams to help tools like this one (and everything else in the pipeline) be as valuable as possible. 

If any additional use cases or requests come to mind, I would absolutely love to hear them. Reach out to me on LinkedIn or X, and I’ll see what we can do.
